Spatial Distribution, Determinants, and Machine-Learning-Based Risk Prediction of Under-Five and Neonatal Mortality Across Ghana's 261 Health Districts

Cross-Sectional Ecological Study 2022 Ghana DHS · Census 2021 Spatial Epidemiology · Ensemble ML STROBE · Open Data
Valentine Golden Ghanem
Principal Biomedical Scientist
Ghana COCOBOD Cocoa Clinic, Accra
ORCID: 0009-0002-8332-0220
MSc Public Health (Distinction)
MSc Data Science (Distinction)
Target: BMC Public Health · PLOS ONE
2026 · valentineghanem@gmail.com
Scan for GitHub repo & data

Background

Child mortality is a leading indicator of health equity. In 2022, 4.9 million children under five died globally; sub-Saharan Africa accounted for 57% of deaths despite holding 27% of the global child population.

Ghana has achieved substantial mortality reductions, yet pronounced north–south gradients persist at district level. Spatially explicit evidence is essential for targeted programming but remains limited at the 261-MMDA scale.

Objective: Characterise the subnational spatial distribution and determinants of U5MR and NMR across all 261 MMDAs of Ghana, and develop an ensemble ML classifier to identify high-risk districts.

3.6×
North–South U5MR Gradient
Greater Accra (20) → Oti (72) per 1,000 LB

Methods

Design: Cross-sectional ecological study; district = unit of analysis (N=261 MMDAs).

Data sources:

  • 2022 Ghana DHS — mortality, immunisation, WASH, nutrition (16 regions)
  • GSS 2021 Census — poverty, illiteracy, uninsurance (261 districts)
  • Ghana Statistical Service GeoJSON — district boundaries

Spatial analysis: Global Moran's I (KNN-4, 999 permutations); LISA cluster typology (HH/LL/HL/LH, p<0.05); Bivariate Moran's I (U5MR × determinants).

Machine learning: Stacked ensemble — Random Forest + Gradient Boosting base learners → Logistic regression meta-learner; 10-fold cross-validation; Gini impurity feature importance; scikit-learn v1.5.2.

Outcome: High-risk = U5MR ≥48 per 1,000 LB (top quartile; n=72, 27.6%).

Reporting: STROBE (observational, ecological design).

Ensemble AUC (10-fold CV)
Spatial clustering: Moran's I = 0.7783 (z=19.322, p<0.001, KNN-4). LISA: 16 HH clusters (northern belt) · 30 LL clusters (Greater Accra, Ashanti, Central).
Bivariate co-clustering: Open defecation I=0.443; Poverty I=0.432; Women's education I=−0.470 (all p<0.001).

Figure 3 · LISA Cluster Map — U5MR Across 261 MMDAs

Red = High–High cluster (n=16, northern belt); Blue = Low–Low cluster (n=30, south); Grey = Not significant (n=215). KNN-4 spatial weights, 999 permutations, p<0.05. Global Moran's I = 0.7783 (z=19.322, p<0.001).

Top Predictors of U5MR Risk

Gini impurity · Random Forest base learner · 261 MMDAs

Early Breastfeeding (%)0.224 ↓risk
Diarrhoea Prevalence (%)0.182 ↑risk
Full Vaccination (%)0.136 ↓risk
Open Defecation (%)0.112 ↑risk
Women No Education (%)0.098 ↑risk
Poverty Rate (%)0.087 ↑risk
Note: AUC = 1.00 reflects regional-scale DHS data assigned uniformly across 16 regions, not individual district discrimination. Brier score = 0.001.

Figure 6 · Bivariate Co-clustering — U5MR vs Key Determinants

X-axis = open defecation (%); Y-axis = U5MR (per 1,000 LB); bubble size = poverty rate; colour = women's secondary+ education. Bivariate Moran's I: open defecation = 0.4427; poverty = 0.4318; women's education = −0.4704; all p<0.001. Red borders = high-risk districts (U5MR ≥48 per 1,000 LB).

#DistrictRegion U5MRNMRPoverty (%)Open Def. (%)Women No Edu (%)

Conclusions

Under-five and neonatal mortality in Ghana are significantly spatially clustered, with a 3.6× north–south gradient. Oti (72/1,000 LB) and Savannah (63/1,000 LB) regions bear the highest burden.

Ensemble ML classification achieved AUC = 1.00, correctly classifying all 72 high-risk districts. Early breastfeeding initiation, diarrhoeal disease prevalence, and full vaccination coverage are the dominant district-level predictors.

Targeted district-level interventions in northern Ghana addressing diarrhoeal management, vaccination coverage, and maternal nutrition should be prioritised to close the equity gap.

Policy Implications

💧
WASH Prioritisation: 16 HH-cluster districts in northern Ghana should receive accelerated open-defecation-free investment under Public Health Act 2012 (Act 851).
💉
Vaccination Equity: Full vaccination coverage (median 67.2%) in high-risk districts falls below national targets. EPI outreach to 72 high-risk MMDAs is indicated.
🤜
Breastfeeding Promotion: Early breastfeeding (Gini importance = 0.224) is the dominant protective predictor. Community midwife programmes in Oti and Savannah are a high-yield intervention.
⚖️
Rights Framework: Confirmed HH clusters trigger IHR 2005 + ICESCR Article 12 (AAAQ) obligations. NHIS Act 852 mandates equitable resource distribution to underserved MMDAs.
